feed.title: Silver Fox Logistics - Vitrual Truck Manager 3

entry.title: Monopolies
entry.summary: What is Monopolis? Monopolis is an activity in which partnerships have the possibility to monopolize transports in the countries they operate. After the event begins, groups of 3–6 partnerships will be formed, and the members of the partnerships will have to establish monopolies on the…
entry.content:
What is Monopolis?

Monopolis is an activity in which partnerships have the possibility to monopolize transports in the countries they operate.

After the event begins, groups of 3–6 partnerships will be formed, and the members of the partnerships will have to establish monopolies on the countries from the continent they are associated with.

The teams of partnerships will be randomly chosen from within the same league.

What are Leagues?

Leagues represent the ranking of partnerships based on the points earned in Monopolis.

If there aren't at least 3 partnerships registered in a higher league, they will move down to the lower league until the minimum number of partnerships for participation is met.

How is the map organized?

Each partnership will be randomly assigned a base in a specific country.

Depending on the league the participating partnerships are in, they will need to obtain a score, which can be increased through local transports to establish a monopoly.

Once the required transports have been completed, a monopoly will be established in that state.

What happens to the states where you have a monopoly?

The state where your base is located will be unoccupied by anyone.

After the monopoly is established, no partnership will be able to load monopoly transports from that state for the next 6 hours (thus, the monopoly is protected for 6 hours).

After the protection expires, any other partnership can establish a monopoly on the state as long as it has a monopoly in a neighboring state.

How will the transports be carried out?

The transports will be strictly local, not international. Thus, the countries that can be occupied must have at least 3 cities. States with fewer than 3 cities (e.g. Luxembourg) cannot be occupied, as there are no local transport possibilities.

Each partnership will have individual transports, and the contracts will only be available to one partnership. Another partnership trying to establish a monopoly in the same state will have separate transports to perform.

What is the stress level?

This indicates your company's stress level in monopoly transports and does not affect any other transports (normal or partnership).

The stress level increases with each transport loaded in each truck, and this will cause the drivers doing monopoly transports to get tired faster, until the point where transports are no longer profitable for a player, and all members of the partnership will need to be influenced to participate in these transports.

The stress index will be reset every 24 hours for all players.

Certain upgrades may reduce the frequency of stress increase.

entry.title: Bank
entry.summary: What is the bank? The name might make you think that you can borrow money from the bank, but unfortunately, the bank doesn't lend you any money—it only takes it! The higher the level of this building, the lower the taxes and leasing costs you…
entry.content:
What is the bank?

The name might make you think that you can borrow money from the bank, but unfortunately, the bank doesn't lend you any money—it only takes it!

The higher the level of this building, the lower the taxes and leasing costs you have to pay.

Tax page

Every week, you must pay taxes. As your company grows, the amount of tax you have to pay will also increase.

On this page, you can see how much tax you owe and when it is due. When the payment deadline arrives, this is the page where you must pay your taxes.

Until your taxes have been paid, you cannot assign your trucks to new routes. Trucks that are already on a route can complete their current deliveries, but they cannot be dispatched on a new route until the taxes have been settled.

Leasing page

If you are leasing any trucks or trailers, they will be listed on this page.

Lease payments are made automatically every day, but you can use this page to repay your leases early. Early repayment reduces the remaining interest by 50%.

If you have leased trucks, the page will display the truck leases by default. You can switch to the trailer lease list using the button in the top-left corner. If you only have leased trailers, the trailer lease list will be displayed automatically.

entry.title: Garage
entry.summary: What is the Garage? The Garage is where you can manage your fleet and buy or lease trucks and trailers. On this page, you can upgrade your Garage. The higher its level, the more trucks and trailers you can own. You can also see a…
entry.content:
What is the Garage?

The Garage is where you can manage your fleet and buy or lease trucks and trailers.

Garage Page

On this page, you can upgrade your Garage. The higher its level, the more trucks and trailers you can own.

You can also see a breakdown of:

  • The number of trailers you own in each category.
  • The number of trucks you own in each star rating.

Trucks Page

Here you can see all of your trucks. You can open the details page for each truck to view information such as its mileage, fuel consumption, engine, fuel tank capacity, towing power, next maintenance, comfort level, and mechanical condition.

If the truck is at your base, you can assign or unassign drivers and trailers. If the truck is currently on a route, you can also view the details of that route.

From this page, you can also:

  • Sell the truck.
  • Register or deregister the truck.
  • Repaint the truck.

Trailers Page

Here you can see all of your trailers. You can view each trailer's type, size, and whether it is currently attached to a truck. If you want to sell a trailer, this is the place to do it.

Buy Page

On this page, you can buy or lease trucks and trailers.

New Trucks

New trucks are the highest-quality vehicles in the game. They are all 5-star rated, and you can choose and purchase any model at any time, provided your player level is 130 or higher.

Used Trucks

Here you can buy or lease an unlimited number of 1-star trucks.

In addition, every hour a new selection of higher-star used trucks becomes available. These trucks are visible to all players on the server, so the first player to purchase a truck gets it. You can buy one higher-star truck from these hourly offers once every 24 hours.

Trailers

On this page, you can buy or lease trailers of every available type and size.

entry.title: Headquarters
entry.summary: What is the Headquarter? The Headquarters building is where you can manage your drivers. Each truck can have up to two drivers. A truck can operate with only one driver, but in that case, it will spend a significant part of the day parked while…
entry.content:
What is the Headquarter?

The Headquarters building is where you can manage your drivers.

Each truck can have up to two drivers. A truck can operate with only one driver, but in that case, it will spend a significant part of the day parked while the driver rests.

With two drivers assigned, one driver can rest while the other is driving, allowing the truck to stay on the road much longer. However, resting takes longer than becoming tired, so eventually both drivers will become exhausted, and the truck will have to stop until one of them has recovered enough energy to continue driving.

Headquarters page

On this page, you can upgrade your Headquarters building. The higher the building's level, the more drivers you can employ.

This page shows the maximum number of drivers allowed at your current building level, as well as the limit for the next level. It also displays the total number of drivers you currently have, along with a breakdown of how many drivers you have at each level.

Drivers page

On this page, you can see all of your drivers. For each driver, the following information is displayed:

  • The driver's name
  • The total distance they have driven
  • Their star rating
  • The license plate of the truck they are assigned to

If you click or tap on a driver, their profile page will open. Here you can see the same information as before, along with additional details about their assigned truck, trailer, and current route.

At the bottom of the page, there are two buttons: Train and Fire.

Use the Fire button if you want to dismiss the driver. The Train button allows you to improve the driver's abilities. As they gain experience and reach certain driving distance milestones, they can be trained to earn higher star ratings or unlock special skills, allowing them to transport fragile, flammable, or oversized cargo.

Hire page

On this page, you can hire new drivers.

You can hire an unlimited number of 1-star drivers. Simply click or tap on the 1-star driver, enter the number of drivers you want to hire, and then press the Hire button.

You can also spend VTP to hire drivers of any star level without any restrictions.

Every hour, a new group of higher-star drivers becomes available for hire. These drivers are visible to all players on the server, so the first player to hire a driver gets it. You can hire a higher-star driver from these hourly offers only once every 24 hours.

entry.title: Café
entry.summary: What is the Café? This is a building where you can see your friends and you can run a little café in the game. There are two categories of members in the game: Friends and Guests. The difference between them can be confusing at first.
entry.content:
What is the Café?

This is a building where you can see your friends and you can run a little café in the game.

Friends and Guests – What's the Difference?

There are two categories of members in the game: Friends and Guests. The difference between them can be confusing at first.

You can have as many Friends as you want. Friends do not directly affect your Café or provide any special benefits. Guests, however, are Friends whom you have invited to your Café. The number of Guests you can have is limited and depends on your Café's level.

Table Page

Guests can visit and sit in your Café. For each seated Guest, you earn Coffee Points, which can be used to purchase various bonuses.

After a Guest visits a Café, they must wait 24 hours before they can visit again. Similarly, when you invite someone to become a Guest, they must wait 24 hours after accepting the invitation before their first visit.

To maximize the number of Coffee Points you earn, it is best to collect them only when your Café is full. The reward increases with the number of seated Guests. For example:

  • 1 Guest = 1 Coffee Point
  • 2 Guests = 3 Coffee Points
  • Additional Guests continue to increase the reward

You can collect Coffee Points either with or without watching an advertisement. Watching an advertisement grants additional Coffee Points, allowing you to earn rewards faster.

Friends Page

This page displays a list of your Guests. It shows when they last visited your Café and when you last visited theirs, or whether you are currently able to visit them.

To visit a friend's Café, simply click or tap the blue "Visit Coffee Shop" button next to their name. You can only visit if your friend has an available seat in their Café. If their Café is full, you will receive the message: "Your friend's coffee shop is already full!" In that case, try again later.

In the top-left corner, there is a button that switches the view to a list of players who are not currently your Guests. This list includes players who are already your Friends, as well as players who have sent you a friend request.

If a player is already your Friend, you can invite them to your Café or accept their invitation to join yours. An invitation can only be sent or accepted if both players still have free space available in their Guest lists.

Redeem Page

This page displays the bonuses that can be redeemed using Coffee Points.

Hurry Up the Trucks

For Coffee Points, you can instantly advance all of your trucks by 250 km, 450 km, or 650 km. To get the most benefit from this bonus, make sure that most of your trucks are neither parked in a city nor about to arrive at their destinations.

Energy for Drivers

This boost helps your drivers become tired more slowly, allowing them to stay active for longer periods.

Premium Packages

Premium Speed
Increases the speed of all your trucks by 10% for 24 hours.

Premium Drivers
Automatically replaces drivers whose energy reaches 0%, keeping your trucks moving without interruption for 24 hours.

Premium Table
Grants access to the Premium Table feature for 24 hours. See the section below the Dispatcher page for more information.

entry.title: Partnership Building
entry.summary: What is the Partnership? Every activity that we do together is located in the Partnership building. This inclides: Monopolis, Season, Members, Wildcards, Contracts, Auctions. Every player who want to play with other has to build the Partnership building to level 1. Only the Partnership keeper…
entry.content:
What is the Partnership?

Every activity that we do together is located in the Partnership building. This inclides: Monopolis, Season, Members, Wildcards, Contracts, Auctions.

Who should build it?

Every player who want to play with other has to build the Partnership building to level 1. Only the Partnership keeper has to upgrade it in order to expand the Partnership and to be able to have more members.

Partnership page

On this page, you can upgrade the building and join to the Monopolis event. Monopolis is the map on the bottom, about this you can read in another post.

Season page

On this page, you can see a list of awards that the Partnership can earn after reaching certain production milestones. When you qualify for an award, you can claim it. The award must be claimed before the end of the season; otherwise, it will be lost when the new season starts.

Members page

On this page, you can see the members of the Partnership. You can view each member's level, the number of trucks they own, and the points they have earned during the current season. If you have permission to manage members, you can do so from this page.

Wildcards page

On this page, you can see the wildcards that can boost the Partnership's XP. Wildcards can be earned as seasonal rewards and donated by members. Once enough wildcards have been donated, the booster can be activated.

Contracts

On this page, you can see the contracts won by the Partnership. At the top, you can view the active contracts, while completed and failed contracts are displayed at the bottom.

For each active contract, you can see the loading and unloading cities, the name of the cargo, and the trailer types that can be used for the contract. The contract details show the total amount of cargo required and the percentage that has already been completed.

Below this information, you can see the reward for completing the contract and the penalties that apply if the contract fails. You can also view the remaining time available to complete the contract and the distance between the two cities.

At the bottom of each contract panel, there is a progress bar with two buttons. The left button displays a list of members currently working on the contract, along with the number of trucks they have assigned to it. The right button shows a list of members working on the contract and the amount of cargo they have delivered so far.

Auction

On this page, you can see the auctions that are currently available. The auction panels are similar to the contract panels, with a Bid button at the bottom.

Each auction has an Open In timer. When the timer reaches zero, the Partnership that placed the last bid wins the auction and can start working on the contract.

Tip: Currently, most Partnerships wait until the final moments of an auction before placing their bids. If you bid too early, another Partnership will likely outbid you. For the best chance of winning, try to be online during the last few seconds of the auction, monitor the bidding activity, and place your bid at the right moment.

entry.title: The mini-game
entry.summary: What is Transport Workers? Transport Workers is a separate activity from the rest of the Virtual Truck Manager game, which can help you develop the main game. You need to use the workers from each transport category to generate as much money as possible. Click…
entry.content:
What is Transport Workers?

Transport Workers is a separate activity from the rest of the Virtual Truck Manager game, which can help you develop the main game.

How to play?

You need to use the workers from each transport category to generate as much money as possible.

Click on each worker to start working, and when the worker finishes their job, the generated money will go into the mini-game account, and then the workers can start working again.

Using a sum of money, you can hire dispatchers who will automate the activity.

Each worker can be upgraded to produce more money. Every 10 levels, the worker becomes faster.

What is the goal of the game?

The ultimate goal is to buy back planes. These planes start at $1,000,000, and the price of each subsequent plane increases tenfold.

Progress can be burned to reset the price of the planes, and the planes already purchased will remain in the account.

Purchased planes can be used to receive various benefits in the main game.

Where is the mini-game?

The game has a small icon in the top-left corner with a picture of a van on it.

Tips for playing

To increase your income, you can watch advertisements. Each ad doubles your income for 2 hours. This bonus can be stacked, so every ad adds an additional 2 hours to the duration. You can also watch an ad to receive a 1-hour bonus income boost.

Once you buy a plane for $1M, it is best to reset the game. You can find the reset option under the flame icon in the bottom-right corner in the redeem menu.

You do not need to pull too much. In my experience, it is enough to upgrade only the first truck to level 20, purchase the dispatcher, and let it generate income until you reach $1M. If you watch ads regularly, you can reach this level in about 3–4 hours.

Income and upgrade costs are randomized. Start the truck by clicking or tapping until you earn enough money for the first upgrade. If the income does not reach $18 (with the double-income bonus active), it is best to reset the game immediately and start over. If you get $18 or more, continue upgrading until you reach  $50, buy the dispatcher, and then upgrade it from time to time until it reaches level 20.
